name: Trivy-Style CI/CD Version Tag Mutation, Secret Harvest, and Artifact Poisoning
objective: Validate detection and response coverage for a CI/CD supply chain attack where a trusted tool tag is mutated to deliver a malicious payload that harvests secrets, poisons artifacts, and attempts cloud pivoting.

steps.title: Obtain CI token
steps.description: Exploit CI/CD misconfiguration or prior compromise to obtain a privileged service account token.

steps.title: Access release pipeline
steps.description: Use the token to access the release automation environment and enumerate trusted tags and workflows.

steps.title: Mutate trusted tag
steps.description: Force-push malicious code to an existing trusted version tag rather than creating a new suspicious release.

steps.title: Trigger downstream build
steps.description: Allow dependent pipelines to pull the poisoned action or artifact during normal build execution.

steps.title: Harvest SSH keys
steps.description: Enumerate runner filesystem locations and access SSH keys, tokens, cloud credential files, and kubeconfigs.

steps.title: Detect malicious build behavior
steps.description: Blue team validates alerts for forced tag mutation, secret file access, and anomalous runner egress.

steps.title: Contain pipeline
steps.description: Disable runners, pause active workflows, and revoke compromised CI/CD tokens and app installations.

steps.title: Rotate secrets
steps.description: Rotate exposed AWS, Azure, GCP, GitHub, Docker, and Kubernetes credentials accessible to affected runners.

steps.title: Validate artifact trust
steps.description: Rebuild from trusted commits, verify signatures, compare SBOMs, and block promotion of exposure-window artifacts.

steps.title: Check cloud pivot
steps.description: Investigate attempted use of exposed credentials for AWS or cloud control-plane access.

steps.title: Executive escalation
steps.description: SOC manager opens P1 incident, coordinates downstream notifications, and publishes internal advisory.

steps.title: Lessons learned
steps.description: Capture detection gaps, enforce SHA pinning, ephemeral runners, signing, and OIDC-only cloud access as corrective actions.

success_criteria: Forced tag mutation detected within 5 minutes
Secret-access or runner-behavior alert generated during build execution
All affected secrets rotated within 2 hours
No exposure-window artifact promoted to production
P1 incident and downstream advisory issued within 1 hour
lab_safety_policy: SIMULATE mode only | No production systems affected
